title: pulmonaria angustifolia subsp. azurea
intro: Pulmonaria angustifolia subsp. azurea, also known as Lungwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, Lungwort has been used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which have been found to be beneficial for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, Lungwort is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to relieve respiratory symptoms and support overall lung health.
